398: function atomicWriteJson(filePath: string, value: unknown): void {
399:   const dir = path.dirname(filePath);
400:   const tempPath = path.join(
401:     dir,
402:     `.${path.basename(filePath)}.${process.pid}.${Date.now()}.tmp`,
403:   );
404:   const data = `${JSON.stringify(value, null, 2)}\n`;
405:   fsSync.writeFileSync(tempPath, data, 'utf8');
406:   fsSync.renameSync(tempPath, filePath);
407: }
408: 
409: function getNodeErrorCode(error: unknown): string | null {
410:   return typeof error === 'object' && error !== null && 'code' in error
411:     ? String((error as { code?: unknown }).code)
412:     : null;
413: }
414: 
415: function getCanonicalSaveLockOwnerState(lockPath: string): CanonicalSaveLockOwnerState {
416:   let ownerRaw: string;
417:   try {
418:     ownerRaw = fsSync.readFileSync(path.join(lockPath, 'owner'), 'utf8');
419:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
420:     return 'unknown';
421:   }
422: 
423:   const ownerPid = Number(ownerRaw.split(/\r?\n/, 1)[0]?.trim());
424:   if (!Number.isInteger(ownerPid) || ownerPid <= 0) {
425:     return 'unknown';
426:   }
427: 
428:   try {
429:     process.kill(ownerPid, 0);
430:     return 'alive';
431:   } catch (error: unknown) {
432:     const code = getNodeErrorCode(error);
433:     if (code === 'ESRCH') {
434:       return 'dead';
435:     }
436:     if (code === 'EPERM') {
437:       return 'alive';
438:     }
439:     return 'unknown';
440:   }
441: }
442: 
443: function ownsCanonicalSaveLock(lockPath: string): boolean {
444:   try {
445:     const ownerRaw = fsSync.readFileSync(path.join(lockPath, 'owner'), 'utf8');
446:     const ownerPid = Number(ownerRaw.split(/\r?\n/, 1)[0]?.trim());
447:     return Number.isInteger(ownerPid) && ownerPid === process.pid;
448:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
449:     return false;
450:   }
451: }
452: 
453: function stopCanonicalSaveLockHeartbeat(lockPath: string): void {
454:   const heartbeat = canonicalSaveHeartbeats.get(lockPath);
455:   if (!heartbeat) {
456:     return;
457:   }
458:   clearInterval(heartbeat);
459:   canonicalSaveHeartbeats.delete(lockPath);
460: }
461: 
462: function startCanonicalSaveLockHeartbeat(lockPath: string): void {
463:   stopCanonicalSaveLockHeartbeat(lockPath);
464:   const heartbeat = setInterval(() => {
465:     try {
466:       const now = new Date();
467:       fsSync.utimesSync(lockPath, now, now);
468:     } catch (_error: unknown) {
469:       stopCanonicalSaveLockHeartbeat(lockPath);
470:     }
471:   }, CANONICAL_SAVE_HEARTBEAT_MS);
472:   const unref = (heartbeat as { unref?: () => void }).unref;
473:   unref?.call(heartbeat);
474:   canonicalSaveHeartbeats.set(lockPath, heartbeat);
475: }
476: 
477: function createCanonicalSaveLock(lockPath: string): string {
478:   // mkdir is the atomic acquire gate: it fails EEXIST when a lock dir already
479:   // exists, so the caller's age/owner-state reclaim logic stays authoritative
480:   // (an empty dir must not be silently reclaimed outside that gate). The owner
481:   // record is then written via a temp file renamed into place so a visible lock
482:   // dir converges to having an owner without a partial-write artifact.
483:   fsSync.mkdirSync(lockPath);
484:   const ownerTmp = path.join(lockPath, `.owner.${process.pid}.tmp`);
485:   fsSync.writeFileSync(ownerTmp, `${process.pid}\n${new Date().toISOString()}\n`, 'utf8');
486:   fsSync.renameSync(ownerTmp, path.join(lockPath, 'owner'));
487:   startCanonicalSaveLockHeartbeat(lockPath);
488:   return lockPath;
489: }
490: 
491: function acquireCanonicalSaveLock(specFolderPath: string | null): string | null {
492:   if (!specFolderPath) {
493:     return null;
494:   }
495: 
496:   const lockPath = path.join(specFolderPath, CANONICAL_SAVE_LOCK_DIR);
497:   try {
498:     return createCanonicalSaveLock(lockPath);
499:   } catch (error: unknown) {
500:     if (!fsSync.existsSync(lockPath)) {
501:       throw error;
502:     }
503:     const stat = fsSync.statSync(lockPath);
504:     const ageMs = Date.now() - stat.mtimeMs;
505:     const ownerState = getCanonicalSaveLockOwnerState(lockPath);
506:     if (ownerState === 'dead' || (ownerState === 'unknown' && ageMs > CANONICAL_SAVE_STALE_MS)) {
507:       console.warn(`Warning: removing abandoned canonical save lock (${ownerState}, ${Math.round(ageMs)}ms): ${lockPath}`);
508:       fsSync.rmSync(lockPath, { recursive: true, force: true });
509:       const reclaimed = createCanonicalSaveLock(lockPath);
510:       // Reap-then-create is not a single atomic step, so a second reaper could
511:       // have published its own lock in the gap. Confirm we actually own the
512:       // visible lock; if another pid won, release our heartbeat and fail closed.
513:       if (!ownsCanonicalSaveLock(lockPath)) {
514:         stopCanonicalSaveLockHeartbeat(reclaimed);
515:         throw new Error(`Canonical save lock is active: ${lockPath}`);
516:       }
517:       return reclaimed;
518:     }
519:     throw new Error(`Canonical save lock is active: ${lockPath}`);
520:   }
521: }
522: 
523: function releaseCanonicalSaveLock(lockPath: string | null): void {
524:   if (!lockPath) {
525:     return;
526:   }
527:   stopCanonicalSaveLockHeartbeat(lockPath);
528:   fsSync.rmSync(lockPath, { recursive: true, force: true });
529: }
530: 
531: function getPacketIdFromGraphMetadata(specFolderPath: string): string {
532:   const graphFile = path.join(specFolderPath, GRAPH_METADATA_FILE);
533:   try {
534:     const metadata = readGraphMetadata(graphFile);
535:     if (typeof metadata.packet_id === 'string' && metadata.packet_id.trim().length > 0) {
536:       return metadata.packet_id;
537:     }
538:   } catch {
539:     // Fall through to path-derived packet id.
540:   }
541: 
542:   for (const specsDir of getSpecsDirectories()) {
543:     const relative = path.relative(path.resolve(specsDir), path.resolve(specFolderPath));
544:     if (relative && !relative.startsWith('..') && !path.isAbsolute(relative)) {
545:       return relative.replace(/\\/g, '/');
546:     }
547:   }
548: 
549:   return path.basename(specFolderPath);
550: }
551: 
552: export function updatePhaseParentPointer(
553:   phaseParentPath: string,
554:   activeChildId: string | null,
555:   timestamp: string,
556: ): void {
557:   const graphFile = path.join(phaseParentPath, GRAPH_METADATA_FILE);
558: 
559:   // Validate the existing file against the schema before mutating it so that
560:   // pre-existing drift is caught eagerly rather than silently propagated.
561:   const raw = fsSync.readFileSync(graphFile, 'utf8');
562:   const metadata: GraphMetadata = graphMetadataSchema.parse(JSON.parse(raw));
563: 
564:   // Build the updated derived block as a typed value so the compiler enforces
565:   // the field constraints (e.g. last_active_at must be an offset-aware ISO 8601
566:   // string, last_active_child_id must be a non-empty string or null).
567:   const updatedDerived: GraphMetadata['derived'] = {
568:     ...metadata.derived,
569:     last_active_child_id: activeChildId,
570:     last_active_at: timestamp,
571:     // Bump last_save_at so consumers reading derived metadata see a current save timestamp.
572:     last_save_at: timestamp,
573:   };
574: 
575:   // Ensure children_ids contains the saved child's packet id
576:   // (idempotent — only inserts when absent so concurrent saves stay safe).
577:   const updatedChildrenIds: string[] = activeChildId && !metadata.children_ids.includes(activeChildId)
578:     ? [...metadata.children_ids, activeChildId]
579:     : metadata.children_ids;
580: 
581:   const updated: GraphMetadata = {
582:     ...metadata,
583:     children_ids: updatedChildrenIds,
584:     derived: updatedDerived,
585:   };
586: 
587:   atomicWriteJson(graphFile, updated);
588: }
589: 
590: export function updatePhaseParentPointersAfterSave(
591:   specFolderPath: string,
592:   timestamp: string = new Date().toISOString(),
593: ): void {
594:   const resolvedSpecFolder = path.resolve(specFolderPath);
595: 
596:   if (isPhaseParent(resolvedSpecFolder)) {
597:     updatePhaseParentPointer(resolvedSpecFolder, null, timestamp);
598:     return;
599:   }
600: 
601:   const directParent = path.dirname(resolvedSpecFolder);
602:   if (directParent !== resolvedSpecFolder && isPhaseParent(directParent)) {
603:     updatePhaseParentPointer(
604:       directParent,
605:       getPacketIdFromGraphMetadata(resolvedSpecFolder),
606:       timestamp,
607:     );
608:   }
609: }
610: 
611: async function readAllStdin(stdin: NodeJS.ReadStream = process.stdin): Promise<string> {
612:   return await new Promise<string>((resolve, reject) => {
613:     let buffer = '';
614:     stdin.setEncoding('utf8');
615:     stdin.on('data', (chunk: string) => {
616:       buffer += chunk;
617:     });
618:     stdin.on('end', () => resolve(buffer));
619:     stdin.on('error', reject);
620:   });
621: }
622: 
623: function parseStructuredJson(rawJson: string, sourceLabel: '--stdin' | '--json'): Record<string, unknown> {
624:   if (rawJson.trim().length === 0) {
625:     throw new Error(`${sourceLabel} requires a non-empty JSON object`);
626:   }
627: 
628:   let parsed: unknown;
629:   try {
630:     parsed = JSON.parse(rawJson);
631:   } catch (error: unknown) {
632:     const errMsg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
633:     throw new Error(`Invalid JSON provided via ${sourceLabel}: ${errMsg}`);
634:   }
635: 
636:   if (typeof parsed !== 'object' || parsed === null || Array.isArray(parsed)) {
637:     throw new Error(`${sourceLabel} requires a JSON object payload`);
638:   }
639: 
640:   return parsed as Record<string, unknown>;
641: }
642: 
643: async function parseStructuredModeArguments(
644:   mode: '--stdin' | '--json',
645:   args: string[],
646:   stdinReader: (stdin?: NodeJS.ReadStream) => Promise<string>,
647: ): Promise<ParsedCliArguments> {
648:   if (mode === '--stdin' && process.stdin.isTTY) {
649:     console.error('--stdin requires piped input');
650:     process.exit(1);
651:   }
652: 
653:   const rawJson = mode === '--stdin'
654:     ? await stdinReader()
655:     : args[1];
656: 
657:   if (mode === '--json' && rawJson === undefined) {
658:     throw new Error('--json requires an inline JSON string argument');
659:   }
660: 
661:   // Validation: empty --json is a user error, not a crash
662:   if (mode === '--json' && (rawJson === undefined || rawJson.trim().length === 0)) {
663:     console.log(JSON.stringify({ error: 'VALIDATION_ERROR', message: 'Empty --json input' }));
664:     process.exit(1);
665:   }
666: 
667:   const payload = parseStructuredJson(rawJson ?? '', mode);
668:   const explicitSpecFolderArg = args[mode === '--stdin' ? 1 : 2];
669:   const explicitTarget = explicitSpecFolderArg ? resolveCliSpecFolderReference(explicitSpecFolderArg) || explicitSpecFolderArg : null;
670:   const payloadTargetRaw = extractPayloadSpecFolder(payload);
671:   const payloadTarget = payloadTargetRaw ? (resolveCliSpecFolderReference(payloadTargetRaw) || payloadTargetRaw) : null;
672:   const specFolderArg = explicitTarget || payloadTarget;
673: 
674:   if (!specFolderArg) {
675:     throw new Error(`${mode} requires a target spec folder via an explicit CLI override or payload specFolder`);
676:   }
677: 
678:   return {
679:     dataFile: null,
680:     specFolderArg,
681:     collectedData: {
682:       ...payload,
683:       _source: 'file',
684:     },
685:     sessionId: null,
686:     plannerMode: 'plan-only',
687:   };
688: }
689: 
690: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
691: // 5. CLI ARGUMENT PARSING
692: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
693: async function parseArguments(
694:   argv: string[] = process.argv.slice(2),
695:   stdinReader: (stdin?: NodeJS.ReadStream) => Promise<string> = readAllStdin,
696: ): Promise<ParsedCliArguments> {
697:   // Extract --session-id <uuid> from argv before positional parsing
698:   let sessionId: string | null = null;
699:   let plannerMode: 'plan-only' | 'full-auto' | 'hybrid' = 'plan-only';
700:   const filteredArgv: string[] = [];
701:   for (let i = 0; i < argv.length; i++) {
702:     if (argv[i] === '--session-id') {
703:       const candidate = argv[i + 1];
704:       if (!candidate || candidate.startsWith('--') || candidate.trim().length === 0) {
705:         throw new Error('--session-id requires a non-empty value');
706:       }
707:       sessionId = candidate.trim();
708:       i++; // skip the value
709:       continue;
710:     }
711:     if (argv[i] === '--planner-mode') {
712:       const candidate = argv[i + 1]?.trim().toLowerCase();
713:       if (!candidate || !['plan-only', 'full-auto', 'hybrid'].includes(candidate)) {
714:         throw new Error('--planner-mode requires one of: plan-only, full-auto, hybrid');
715:       }
716:       plannerMode = candidate as 'plan-only' | 'full-auto' | 'hybrid';
717:       i++;
718:       continue;
719:     }
720:     if (argv[i] === '--full-auto') {
721:       plannerMode = 'full-auto';
722:       continue;
723:     }
724:     filteredArgv.push(argv[i]);
725:   }
726: 
727:   const [primaryArg, secondaryArg] = filteredArgv;
728:   if (!primaryArg) {
729:     return { dataFile: null, specFolderArg: null, collectedData: null, sessionId, plannerMode };
730:   }
731: 
732:   if (primaryArg === '--stdin' || primaryArg === '--json') {
733:     const structured = await parseStructuredModeArguments(primaryArg, filteredArgv, stdinReader);
734:     return { ...structured, sessionId, plannerMode };
735:   }
736: 
737:   const resolvedSpecFolder = resolveCliSpecFolderReference(primaryArg);
738:   if (resolvedSpecFolder) {
739:     throw new Error(
740:       'Direct spec folder mode is no longer supported. ' +
741:       'Use structured JSON via --json, --stdin, or a JSON temp file.'
742:     );
743:   }
744: 
745:   return {
746:     dataFile: primaryArg,
747:     specFolderArg: secondaryArg || null,
748:     collectedData: null,
749:     sessionId,
750:     plannerMode,
751:   };
752: }
753: 
754: function validateArguments(): void {
755:   const specFolderArg = CONFIG.SPEC_FOLDER_ARG;
756:   if (!specFolderArg) return;
757: 
758:   const validation = isValidSpecFolder(specFolderArg);
759: 
760:   if (validation.warning) {
761:     console.warn(`   Warning: ${validation.warning}`);
762:   }
763: 
764:   if (validation.valid) return;
765: 
766:   // --- Subfolder support: before failing, try to find the folder as a child ---
767:   // Only use basename-based child resolution when the input is a single
768:   // segment (no '/' separators). Multi-segment paths like "003-parent/121-child"
769:   // must NOT be silently retargeted by basename alone, because stripping the parent
770:   // could resolve to a different parent's child with the same leaf name.
771:   const inputBaseName = path.basename(specFolderArg);
772:   const isMultiSegment = specFolderArg.includes('/');
773:   if (!isMultiSegment && SPEC_FOLDER_PATTERN.test(inputBaseName)) {
774:     // Input looks like a valid spec folder name but wasn't found at top level.
775:     // Try finding it as a child folder inside any parent.
776:     const resolved = findChildFolderSync(inputBaseName);
777:     if (resolved) {
778:       console.error(`   Resolved child folder "${inputBaseName}" → ${resolved}`);
779:       CONFIG.SPEC_FOLDER_ARG = resolved;
780:       return;
781:     }
782:     // FindChildFolder logs its own error for ambiguous matches
783:   }
784: 
785:   console.error(`\nError: Invalid spec folder format: ${specFolderArg}`);
786:   console.error(`   Reason: ${validation.reason}`);
787:   console.error('Expected format: ###-feature-name (e.g., "122-skill-standardization")\n');
788: 
789:   const specsDir = findActiveSpecsDir() || path.join(CONFIG.PROJECT_ROOT, 'specs');
790:   if (fsSync.existsSync(specsDir)) {
791:     try {
792:       const available = fsSync.readdirSync(specsDir);
793:       const matches = available.filter((n) => n.includes(path.basename(specFolderArg)) && SPEC_FOLDER_PATTERN.test(n));
794: 
795:       if (matches.length > 0) {
796:         console.error('Did you mean:');
797:         matches.forEach((m) => console.error(`  - ${m}`));
798:       } else {
799:         // --- Subfolder support: multi-level deep scan as fallback ---
800:         let deepMatches: string[] = [];
801:         const targetBase = path.basename(specFolderArg);
802: 
803:         for (const topEntry of available) {
804:           const isSpec = SPEC_FOLDER_PATTERN.test(topEntry);
805:           const isCategory = CATEGORY_FOLDER_PATTERN.test(topEntry);
806:           if (!isSpec && !isCategory) continue;
807:           const topPath = path.join(specsDir, topEntry);
808:           try {
809:             if (!fsSync.statSync(topPath).isDirectory()) continue;
810:             const topChildren = fsSync.readdirSync(topPath);
811:             // Search direct children of spec/category folders
812:             const childMatches = topChildren.filter(
813:               (c) => c.includes(targetBase) && SPEC_FOLDER_PATTERN.test(c)
814:             );
815:             for (const child of childMatches) {
816:               deepMatches.push(`${topEntry}/${child}`);
817:             }
818:             // For category folders, also search grandchildren (category/parent/child)
819:             if (isCategory) {
820:               for (const midEntry of topChildren) {
821:                 if (!SPEC_FOLDER_PATTERN.test(midEntry)) continue;
822:                 const midPath = path.join(topPath, midEntry);
823:                 try {
824:                   if (!fsSync.statSync(midPath).isDirectory()) continue;
825:                   const grandChildren = fsSync.readdirSync(midPath);
826:                   const gcMatches = grandChildren.filter(
827:                     (c) => c.includes(targetBase) && SPEC_FOLDER_PATTERN.test(c)
828:                   );
829:                   for (const gc of gcMatches) {
830:                     deepMatches.push(`${topEntry}/${midEntry}/${gc}`);
831:                   }
832:                 } catch (_error: unknown) {
833:                   if (_error instanceof Error) {
834:                     /* skip unreadable dirs */
835:                   }
836:                 }
837:               }
838:             }
839:           } catch (_error: unknown) {
840:             if (_error instanceof Error) {
841:               /* skip unreadable dirs */
842:             }
843:           }
844:         }
845: 
846:         if (deepMatches.length > 0) {
847:           console.error('Did you mean (in subfolders):');
848:           deepMatches.forEach((m) => console.error(`  - ${m}`));
849:         } else {
850:           const allSpecs = available.filter((n) => SPEC_FOLDER_PATTERN.test(n) && !n.match(/^z_|archive/i))
851:                                     .sort().reverse().slice(0, 5);
852:           if (allSpecs.length) {
853:             console.error('Available spec folders:');
854:             allSpecs.forEach((f) => console.error(`  - ${f}`));
855:           }
856:         }
857:       }
858:     } catch (error: unknown) {
859:       const errMsg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
860:       console.error('[generate-context] Failed to list spec folders:', errMsg);
861:     }
862:   }
863:   console.error('\nUsage: node generate-context.js [--stdin [spec-folder-name] | --json <json> [spec-folder-name] | <data-file> [spec-folder-name]]\n');
864:   process.exit(1);
865: }
866: 
867: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
868: // 6. MAIN ENTRY POINT
869: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
870: async function main(
871:   argv: string[] = process.argv.slice(2),
872:   stdinReader: (stdin?: NodeJS.ReadStream) => Promise<string> = readAllStdin,
873: ): Promise<void> {
874:   console.error('Starting memory skill...\n');
875: 
876:   try {
877:     const parsed = await parseArguments(argv, stdinReader);
878:     CONFIG.DATA_FILE = parsed.dataFile;
879:     CONFIG.SPEC_FOLDER_ARG = parsed.specFolderArg;
880:     validateArguments();
881:     const lockedSpecFolder = resolveExistingSpecFolderPath(CONFIG.SPEC_FOLDER_ARG);
882:     const canonicalSaveLock = acquireCanonicalSaveLock(lockedSpecFolder);
883: 
884:     try {
885:       await runWorkflow({
886:         dataFile: parsed.collectedData ? undefined : CONFIG.DATA_FILE ?? undefined,
887:         specFolderArg: CONFIG.SPEC_FOLDER_ARG ?? undefined,
888:         collectedData: parsed.collectedData ?? undefined,
889:         loadDataFn: parsed.collectedData
890:           ? undefined
891:           : () => loadCollectedData({}),
892:         collectSessionDataFn: collectSessionData,
893:         sessionId: parsed.sessionId ?? undefined,
894:         plannerMode: parsed.plannerMode,
895:       });
896: 
897:       const savedSpecFolder = resolveExistingSpecFolderPath(CONFIG.SPEC_FOLDER_ARG);
898:       if (savedSpecFolder) {
899:         updatePhaseParentPointersAfterSave(savedSpecFolder);
900:       }
901:     } finally {
902:       releaseCanonicalSaveLock(canonicalSaveLock);
903:     }
904:   } catch (error: unknown) {
905:     const errMsg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
906:     const isExpected = /Spec folder not found|No spec folders|specs\/ directory|retry attempts|Expected|Invalid JSON provided|requires a target spec folder|requires an inline JSON string|requires? a non-empty JSON object|JSON object payload|no longer supported|session-id requires/.test(errMsg);
907: 
908:     if (isExpected) {
909:       console.error(`\nError: ${errMsg}`);
910:     } else {
911:       console.error('Unexpected Error:', errMsg);
912:       if (error instanceof Error) {
913:         console.error(error.stack);
914:       }
915:     }
916:     process.exit(1);
917:   }
918: }
919: 
920: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
921: // 7. EXPORTS
922: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
923: if (isMainModule(import.meta.url)) {
924:   installSignalHandlers();
925:   main().catch((error: unknown) => {
926:     const errMsg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
927:     console.error(`Fatal error: ${errMsg}`);
928:     if (error instanceof Error) {
929:       console.error(error.stack);
930:     }
931:     process.exit(1);
932:   });
933: }
934: 
935: export {
936:   main,
937:   readAllStdin,
938:   parseArguments,
939:   validateArguments,
940:   isValidSpecFolder,
941:   extractPayloadSpecFolder,
942:   acquireCanonicalSaveLock,
943:   releaseCanonicalSaveLock,
944:   CANONICAL_SAVE_HEARTBEAT_MS,
945:   CANONICAL_SAVE_STALE_MS,
946: };

3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: 
5: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
6: // 1. AST PARSER
7: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
8: import { chunkMarkdown, splitIntoBlocks } from '@spec-kit/shared/lib/structure-aware-chunker';
9: 
10: /** Structured markdown block emitted by parseMarkdownSections(). */
11: export interface ParsedSection {
12:   type: 'heading' | 'code' | 'table' | 'text';
13:   title: string | null;
14:   content: string;
15: }
16: 
17: function extractHeadingTitle(content: string): string | null {
18:   const match = content.match(/^#{1,6}\s+(.*)$/m);
19:   if (!match) {
20:     return null;
21:   }
22:   return match[1]?.trim() ?? null;
23: }
24: 
25: /**
26:  * Parse markdown into structured sections for retrieval-time weighting.
27:  * Uses the shared chunker so code blocks/tables stay atomic.
28:  */
29: export function parseMarkdownSections(markdown: string): ParsedSection[] {
30:   if (typeof markdown !== 'string' || markdown.trim().length === 0) {
31:     return [];
32:   }
33: 
34:   const sections: ParsedSection[] = [];
35:   const blocks = splitIntoBlocks(markdown);
36:   for (const block of blocks) {
37:     sections.push({
38:       type: block.type,
39:       title: block.type === 'heading' ? extractHeadingTitle(block.content) : null,
40:       content: block.content,
41:     });
42:   }
43:   return sections;
44: }
45: 
46: export {
47:   chunkMarkdown,
48:   splitIntoBlocks,
49: };

<content>
1: // ---------------------------------------------------------------
2: // MODULE: Cleanup Index Scope Violations
3: // ---------------------------------------------------------------
4: 
5: import Database from 'better-sqlite3';
6: import { load as loadSqliteVec } from 'sqlite-vec';
7: import * as path from 'path';
8: 
9: import {
10:   GOVERNANCE_AUDIT_ACTIONS,
11:   buildGovernanceLogicalKey,
12:   isIndexableConstitutionalMemoryPath,
13:   recordTierDowngradeAudit,
14:   shouldIndexForMemory,
15: } from '@spec-kit/mcp-server/api';
16: import { acquireDbInstanceLock, releaseDbInstanceLocks } from '@spec-kit/mcp-server/api/db-lock';
17: import { resolveActiveProfileDbPath } from '@spec-kit/shared/embeddings/profile';
18: import { isMainModule } from '../lib/esm-entry.js';
19: 
20: interface CountRow {
21:   count: number;
22: }
23: 
24: interface MemoryIdRow {
25:   id: number;
26:   content_hash: string | null;
27:   spec_folder: string | null;
28:   file_path: string | null;
29:   canonical_file_path: string | null;
30: }
31: 
32: interface CleanupDowngradeRow {
33:   id: number;
34:   spec_folder: string | null;
35:   file_path: string | null;
36:   canonical_file_path: string | null;
37:   anchor_id: string | null;
38:   importance_tier: string | null;
39: }
40: 
41: interface DuplicateRow {
42:   id: number;
43:   created_at: string | null;
44: }
45: 
46: interface ViolationSummary {
47:   constitutionalTotal: number;
48:   constitutionalInFolder: number;
49:   zFutureRows: number;
50:   externalRows: number;
51:   invalidConstitutionalRows: number;
52:   gateEnforcementDuplicates: number;
53: }
54: 
55: interface ApplySummary {
56:   deletedMemoryRows: number;
57:   deletedHistoryRows: number;
58:   deletedLineageRows: number;
59:   deletedVectorRows: number;
60:   deletedFeedbackRows: number;
61:   deletedOtherReferenceRows: number;
62:   deletedEmbeddingCacheRows: number;
63:   downgradedRows: number;
64:   rewrittenFeedbackRows: number;
65:   rewrittenLineageRows: number;
66:   rewrittenMutationLedgerRows: number;
67:   ftsCleanupStrategy: 'memory_index_trigger';
68: }
69: 
70: interface CleanupPlan {
71:   forbiddenIds: number[];
72:   forbiddenContentHashes: string[];
73:   duplicateOldIds: number[];
74:   duplicateSurvivorId: number | null;
75:   downgradeRows: CleanupDowngradeRow[];
76: }
77: 
78: const HELP_TEXT = `
79: cleanup-index-scope-violations — Repair memory index scope and constitutional tier violations
80: 
81: Usage:
82:   node cleanup-index-scope-violations.js --db <path>
83:   node cleanup-index-scope-violations.js --db <path> --apply
84:   node cleanup-index-scope-violations.js --db <path> --verify
85: 
86: Options:
87:   --db        Required unless MEMORY_DB_PATH is set. SQLite database path.
88:   --apply     Execute the cleanup in one transaction
89:   --verify    Report whether the invariants currently hold (exit 0 clean, 1 violations)
90:   --help,-h   Show this help text
91: `;
92: 
93: function readFlagValue(argv: string[], flag: string): string | null {
94:   const index = argv.indexOf(flag);
95:   if (index === -1 || !argv[index + 1]) {
96:     return null;
97:   }
98:   return argv[index + 1];
99: }
100: 
101: function resolveCleanupDbPath(argv: string[]): string {
102:   const explicitDbPath = readFlagValue(argv, '--db');
103:   if (explicitDbPath && explicitDbPath.trim().length > 0) {
104:     return path.resolve(explicitDbPath.trim());
105:   }
106: 
107:   const envDbPath = process.env.MEMORY_DB_PATH?.trim();
108:   if (envDbPath) {
109:     return path.resolve(envDbPath);
110:   }
111: 
112:   throw new Error(
113:     'Missing required database path. Pass --db <path> or set MEMORY_DB_PATH. ' +
114:     `Active-profile default would be: ${resolveActiveProfileDbPath()}`,
115:   );
116: }
117: 
118: function tableExists(database: InstanceType<typeof Database>, tableName: string): boolean {
119:   const row = database
120:     .prepare("SELECT 1 AS present FROM sqlite_master WHERE type = 'table' AND name = ? LIMIT 1")
121:     .get(tableName) as { present?: number } | undefined;
122:   return row?.present === 1;
123: }
124: 
125: function countQuery(database: InstanceType<typeof Database>, sql: string, ...params: unknown[]): number {
126:   const row = database.prepare(sql).get(...params) as CountRow | undefined;
127:   return row?.count ?? 0;
128: }
129: 
130: function rowIndexPath(row: { file_path?: string | null; canonical_file_path?: string | null; spec_folder?: string | null }): string {
131:   return row.canonical_file_path || row.file_path || row.spec_folder || '';
132: }
133: 
134: function isExcludedMemoryIndexRow(row: { file_path?: string | null; canonical_file_path?: string | null; spec_folder?: string | null }): boolean {
135:   return !shouldIndexForMemory(rowIndexPath(row));
136: }
137: 
138: function allMemoryIndexRows(database: InstanceType<typeof Database>): MemoryIdRow[] {
139:   return database.prepare(`
140:     SELECT id, content_hash, spec_folder, file_path, canonical_file_path
141:     FROM memory_index
142:     ORDER BY id
143:   `).all() as MemoryIdRow[];
144: }
145: 
146: function allConstitutionalRows(database: InstanceType<typeof Database>): CleanupDowngradeRow[] {
147:   return database.prepare(`
148:     SELECT id, spec_folder, file_path, canonical_file_path, anchor_id, importance_tier
149:     FROM memory_index
150:     WHERE importance_tier = 'constitutional'
151:     ORDER BY id
152:   `).all() as CleanupDowngradeRow[];
153: }
154: 
155: function collectSummary(database: InstanceType<typeof Database>): ViolationSummary {
156:   const constitutionalRows = allConstitutionalRows(database);
157:   return {
158:     constitutionalTotal: countQuery(
159:       database,
160:       "SELECT COUNT(*) AS count FROM memory_index WHERE importance_tier = 'constitutional'",
161:     ),
162:     constitutionalInFolder: countQuery(
163:       database,
164:       "SELECT COUNT(*) AS count FROM memory_index WHERE importance_tier = 'constitutional' AND file_path LIKE '%/constitutional/%'",
165:     ),
166:     zFutureRows: countQuery(
167:       database,
168:       "SELECT COUNT(*) AS count FROM memory_index WHERE file_path LIKE '%/z_future/%' OR spec_folder LIKE '%z_future%'",
169:     ),
170:     externalRows: countQuery(
171:       database,
172:       "SELECT COUNT(*) AS count FROM memory_index WHERE file_path LIKE '%/external/%'",
173:     ),
174:     invalidConstitutionalRows: constitutionalRows
175:       .filter(row => !isIndexableConstitutionalMemoryPath(rowIndexPath(row)))
176:       .length,
177:     gateEnforcementDuplicates: countQuery(
178:       database,
179:       "SELECT COUNT(*) AS count FROM memory_index WHERE file_path LIKE '%/constitutional/gate-enforcement.md'",
180:     ),
181:   };
182: }
183: 
184: function buildPlan(database: InstanceType<typeof Database>): CleanupPlan {
185:   const forbiddenRows = allMemoryIndexRows(database).filter(isExcludedMemoryIndexRow);
186: 
187:   const duplicateRows = database.prepare(`
188:     SELECT id, created_at
189:     FROM memory_index
190:     WHERE file_path LIKE '%/constitutional/gate-enforcement.md'
191:     ORDER BY created_at DESC, id DESC
192:   `).all() as DuplicateRow[];
193: 
194:   const duplicateSurvivorId = duplicateRows.length > 0 ? duplicateRows[0].id : null;
195:   const duplicateOldIds = duplicateRows.slice(1).map(row => row.id);
196:   const excludedDowngradeIds = new Set<number>([
197:     ...forbiddenRows.map(row => row.id),
198:     ...duplicateOldIds,
199:   ]);
200: 
201:   const downgradeRows = allConstitutionalRows(database)
202:     .filter(row => !isIndexableConstitutionalMemoryPath(rowIndexPath(row)));
203: 
204:   return {
205:     forbiddenIds: forbiddenRows.map(row => row.id),
206:     forbiddenContentHashes: forbiddenRows
207:       .map(row => row.content_hash)
208:       .filter((value): value is string => typeof value === 'string' && value.length > 0),
209:     duplicateOldIds,
210:     duplicateSurvivorId,
211:     downgradeRows: downgradeRows
212:       .filter(row => !excludedDowngradeIds.has(row.id)),
213:   };
214: }
215: 
216: function placeholders(values: readonly number[]): string {
217:   return values.map(() => '?').join(', ');
218: }
219: 
220: function deleteRows(
221:   database: InstanceType<typeof Database>,
222:   tableName: string,
223:   columnClauses: string[],
224:   ids: readonly number[],
225: ): number {
226:   if (ids.length === 0 || !tableExists(database, tableName)) {
227:     return 0;
228:   }
229: 
230:   const clause = columnClauses
231:     .map(column => `${column} IN (${placeholders(ids)})`)
232:     .join(' OR ');
233:   const statement = `DELETE FROM ${tableName} WHERE ${clause}`;
234:   const params = columnClauses.flatMap(() => ids);
235:   return database.prepare(statement).run(...params).changes;
236: }
237: 
238: function deleteRowsByTextMemoryId(
239:   database: InstanceType<typeof Database>,
240:   tableName: string,
241:   columnName: string,
242:   ids: readonly number[],
243: ): number {
244:   if (ids.length === 0 || !tableExists(database, tableName)) {
245:     return 0;
246:   }
247: 
248:   const stringIds = ids.map(String);
249:   const statement = `DELETE FROM ${tableName} WHERE ${columnName} IN (${stringIds.map(() => '?').join(', ')})`;
250:   return database.prepare(statement).run(...stringIds).changes;
251: }
252: 
253: function rewriteDuplicateReferences(
254:   database: InstanceType<typeof Database>,
255:   oldId: number,
256:   survivorId: number,
257: ): Pick<ApplySummary, 'rewrittenFeedbackRows' | 'rewrittenLineageRows' | 'rewrittenMutationLedgerRows'> {
258:   let rewrittenFeedbackRows = 0;
259:   let rewrittenLineageRows = 0;
260:   let rewrittenMutationLedgerRows = 0;
261: 
262:   if (tableExists(database, 'memory_lineage')) {
263:     rewrittenLineageRows += database.prepare(
264:       'UPDATE memory_lineage SET root_memory_id = ? WHERE root_memory_id = ?',
265:     ).run(survivorId, oldId).changes;
266:     rewrittenLineageRows += database.prepare(
267:       'UPDATE memory_lineage SET predecessor_memory_id = ? WHERE predecessor_memory_id = ?',
268:     ).run(survivorId, oldId).changes;
269:     rewrittenLineageRows += database.prepare(
270:       'UPDATE memory_lineage SET superseded_by_memory_id = ? WHERE superseded_by_memory_id = ?',
271:     ).run(survivorId, oldId).changes;
272:   }
273: 
274:   if (tableExists(database, 'feedback_events')) {
275:     rewrittenFeedbackRows += database.prepare(
276:       'UPDATE feedback_events SET memory_id = ? WHERE memory_id = ?',
277:     ).run(String(survivorId), String(oldId)).changes;
278:   }
279: 
280:   return {
281:     rewrittenFeedbackRows,
282:     rewrittenLineageRows,
283:     rewrittenMutationLedgerRows,
284:   };
285: }
286: 
287: function stripDeletedIdsFromMutationLedger(
288:   _database: InstanceType<typeof Database>,
289:   _deletedIds: readonly number[],
290: ): number {
291:   return 0;
292: }
293: 
294: function cleanupEmbeddingCache(
295:   database: InstanceType<typeof Database>,
296:   contentHashes: readonly string[],
297: ): number {
298:   if (contentHashes.length === 0 || !tableExists(database, 'embedding_cache')) {
299:     return 0;
300:   }
301: 
302:   let deletedRows = 0;
303:   const uniqueHashes = Array.from(new Set(contentHashes));
304:   const hasSurvivorStatement = database.prepare(
305:     'SELECT 1 FROM memory_index WHERE content_hash = ? LIMIT 1',
306:   );
307:   const deleteStatement = database.prepare(
308:     'DELETE FROM embedding_cache WHERE content_hash = ?',
309:   );
310: 
311:   for (const contentHash of uniqueHashes) {
312:     const survivor = hasSurvivorStatement.get(contentHash) as { 1?: number } | undefined;
313:     if (survivor) {
314:       continue;
315:     }
316:     deletedRows += deleteStatement.run(contentHash).changes;
317:   }
318: 
319:   return deletedRows;
320: }
321: 
322: function applyCleanup(database: InstanceType<typeof Database>, plan: CleanupPlan): ApplySummary {
323:   const idsToDelete = Array.from(new Set([...plan.forbiddenIds, ...plan.duplicateOldIds]));
324:   const summary: ApplySummary = {
325:     deletedMemoryRows: 0,
326:     deletedHistoryRows: 0,
327:     deletedLineageRows: 0,
328:     deletedVectorRows: 0,
329:     deletedFeedbackRows: 0,
330:     deletedOtherReferenceRows: 0,
331:     deletedEmbeddingCacheRows: 0,
332:     downgradedRows: 0,
333:     rewrittenFeedbackRows: 0,
334:     rewrittenLineageRows: 0,
335:     rewrittenMutationLedgerRows: 0,
336:     ftsCleanupStrategy: 'memory_index_trigger',
337:   };
338: 
339:   if (plan.duplicateSurvivorId !== null) {
340:     for (const oldId of plan.duplicateOldIds) {
341:       const rewritten = rewriteDuplicateReferences(database, oldId, plan.duplicateSurvivorId);
342:       summary.rewrittenFeedbackRows += rewritten.rewrittenFeedbackRows;
343:       summary.rewrittenLineageRows += rewritten.rewrittenLineageRows;
344:       summary.rewrittenMutationLedgerRows += rewritten.rewrittenMutationLedgerRows;
345:     }
346:   }
347: 
348:   summary.rewrittenMutationLedgerRows += stripDeletedIdsFromMutationLedger(database, idsToDelete);
349: 
350:   summary.deletedHistoryRows += deleteRows(database, 'memory_history', ['memory_id'], idsToDelete);
351:   summary.deletedLineageRows += deleteRows(
352:     database,
353:     'memory_lineage',
354:     ['memory_id', 'root_memory_id', 'predecessor_memory_id', 'superseded_by_memory_id'],
355:     idsToDelete,
356:   );
357:   summary.deletedVectorRows += deleteRows(database, 'vec_memories', ['rowid'], idsToDelete);
358:   summary.deletedFeedbackRows += deleteRowsByTextMemoryId(database, 'feedback_events', 'memory_id', idsToDelete);
359: 
360:   summary.deletedOtherReferenceRows += deleteRows(database, 'active_memory_projection', ['root_memory_id', 'active_memory_id'], idsToDelete);
361:   summary.deletedOtherReferenceRows += deleteRowsByTextMemoryId(database, 'batch_learning_log', 'memory_id', idsToDelete);
362:   summary.deletedOtherReferenceRows += deleteRows(database, 'community_assignments', ['memory_id'], idsToDelete);
363:   summary.deletedOtherReferenceRows += deleteRows(database, 'degree_snapshots', ['memory_id'], idsToDelete);
364:   summary.deletedOtherReferenceRows += deleteRows(database, 'learned_feedback_audit', ['memory_id'], idsToDelete);
365:   summary.deletedOtherReferenceRows += deleteRows(database, 'memory_conflicts', ['new_memory_id', 'existing_memory_id'], idsToDelete);
366:   summary.deletedOtherReferenceRows += deleteRows(database, 'memory_corrections', ['original_memory_id', 'correction_memory_id'], idsToDelete);
367:   summary.deletedOtherReferenceRows += deleteRows(database, 'memory_entities', ['memory_id'], idsToDelete);
368:   summary.deletedOtherReferenceRows += deleteRows(database, 'memory_summaries', ['memory_id'], idsToDelete);
369:   summary.deletedOtherReferenceRows += deleteRows(database, 'negative_feedback_events', ['memory_id'], idsToDelete);
370:   summary.deletedOtherReferenceRows += deleteRows(database, 'scoring_observations', ['memory_id'], idsToDelete);
371:   summary.deletedOtherReferenceRows += deleteRows(database, 'session_sent_memories', ['memory_id'], idsToDelete);
372:   summary.deletedOtherReferenceRows += deleteRows(database, 'working_memory', ['memory_id'], idsToDelete);
373: 
374:   if (idsToDelete.length > 0) {
375:     summary.deletedMemoryRows += deleteRows(database, 'memory_index', ['id'], idsToDelete);
376:   }
377: 
378:   if (plan.downgradeRows.length > 0) {
379:     summary.downgradedRows += database.prepare(`
380:       UPDATE memory_index
381:       SET importance_tier = 'important'
382:       WHERE id IN (${placeholders(plan.downgradeRows.map((row) => row.id))})
383:     `).run(...plan.downgradeRows.map((row) => row.id)).changes;
384: 
385:     for (const row of plan.downgradeRows) {
386:       recordTierDowngradeAudit(database, {
387:         action: GOVERNANCE_AUDIT_ACTIONS.TIER_DOWNGRADE_NON_CONSTITUTIONAL_PATH_CLEANUP,
388:         memoryId: row.id,
389:         logicalKey: buildGovernanceLogicalKey(
390:           row.spec_folder,
391:           row.canonical_file_path || row.file_path,
392:           row.anchor_id,
393:         ),
394:         previousTier: row.importance_tier,
395:         nextTier: 'important',
396:         source: 'cleanup_index_scope_violations',
397:         reason: 'cleanup-script bulk normalization',
398:         filePath: row.file_path,
399:         canonicalFilePath: row.canonical_file_path,
400:       });
401:     }
402:   }
403: 
404:   summary.deletedEmbeddingCacheRows += cleanupEmbeddingCache(database, plan.forbiddenContentHashes);
405: 
406:   return summary;
407: }
408: 
409: function printSummary(label: string, summary: ViolationSummary): void {
410:   console.log(`${label}:`);
411:   console.log(`  constitutional_total=${summary.constitutionalTotal}`);
412:   console.log(`  constitutional_in_folder=${summary.constitutionalInFolder}`);
413:   console.log(`  z_future_rows=${summary.zFutureRows}`);
414:   console.log(`  external_rows=${summary.externalRows}`);
415:   console.log(`  invalid_constitutional_rows=${summary.invalidConstitutionalRows}`);
416:   console.log(`  gate_enforcement_rows=${summary.gateEnforcementDuplicates}`);
417: }
418: 
419: function printApplySummary(summary: ApplySummary): void {
420:   console.log('Applied changes:');
421:   console.log(`  deleted_memory_rows=${summary.deletedMemoryRows}`);
422:   console.log(`  deleted_history_rows=${summary.deletedHistoryRows}`);
423:   console.log(`  deleted_lineage_rows=${summary.deletedLineageRows}`);
424:   console.log(`  deleted_vector_rows=${summary.deletedVectorRows}`);
425:   console.log(`  deleted_feedback_rows=${summary.deletedFeedbackRows}`);
426:   console.log(`  deleted_other_reference_rows=${summary.deletedOtherReferenceRows}`);
427:   console.log(`  deleted_embedding_cache_rows=${summary.deletedEmbeddingCacheRows}`);
428:   console.log(`  downgraded_rows=${summary.downgradedRows}`);
429:   console.log(`  rewritten_feedback_rows=${summary.rewrittenFeedbackRows}`);
430:   console.log(`  rewritten_lineage_rows=${summary.rewrittenLineageRows}`);
431:   console.log(`  rewritten_mutation_ledger_rows=${summary.rewrittenMutationLedgerRows}`);
432:   console.log(`  fts_cleanup_strategy=${summary.ftsCleanupStrategy}`);
433: }
434: 
435: async function main(): Promise<void> {
436:   if (process.argv.includes('--help') || process.argv.includes('-h')) {
437:     console.log(HELP_TEXT);
438:     process.exit(0);
439:   }
440: 
441:   const apply = process.argv.includes('--apply');
442:   const verify = process.argv.includes('--verify');
443:   let database: InstanceType<typeof Database> | null = null;
444: 
445:   try {
446:     const dbPath = resolveCleanupDbPath(process.argv.slice(2));
447:     // Single-writer guard: refuse to mutate a database a live daemon (or
448:     // another maintenance run) currently owns — stop the daemon first.
449:     acquireDbInstanceLock(dbPath);
450:     const db = new Database(dbPath);
451:     database = db;
452:     loadSqliteVec(db);
453:     const before = collectSummary(db);
454:     const plan = buildPlan(db);
455: 
456:     printSummary('Before', before);
457:   console.log('Planned actions:');
458:   console.log(`  would_delete_memory_rows=${plan.forbiddenIds.length + plan.duplicateOldIds.length}`);
459:   console.log(`  would_delete_z_future_or_external_rows=${plan.forbiddenIds.length}`);
460:   console.log(`  would_delete_duplicate_gate_rows=${plan.duplicateOldIds.length}`);
461:   console.log(`  would_downgrade_rows=${plan.downgradeRows.length}`);
462:   console.log(`  duplicate_survivor_id=${plan.duplicateSurvivorId ?? 'none'}`);
463: 
464:     if (verify) {
465:       const hasViolations = before.zFutureRows > 0
466:         || before.externalRows > 0
467:         || before.invalidConstitutionalRows > 0
468:         || before.gateEnforcementDuplicates > 1;
469:       process.exit(hasViolations ? 1 : 0);
470:     }
471: 
472:     if (!apply) {
473:       console.log('Dry-run only. Re-run with --apply to mutate the database.');
474:       process.exit(0);
475:     }
476: 
477:     const tx = db.transaction(() => {
478:       const transactionPlan = buildPlan(db);
479:       const applied = applyCleanup(db, transactionPlan);
480:       const after = collectSummary(db);
481:       return { applied, after };
482:     });
483:     const { applied, after } = tx();
484: 
485:     printApplySummary(applied);
486:     printSummary('After', after);
487:     process.exit(0);
488:   } catch (error: unknown) {
489:     console.error('[cleanup-index-scope-violations] Error:', error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error));
490:     process.exit(1);
491:   } finally {
492:     database?.close();
493:     releaseDbInstanceLocks();
494:   }
495: }
496: 
497: if (isMainModule(import.meta.url)) {
498:   void main();
499: }
500: 
501: export { applyCleanup, buildPlan, collectSummary, main };

(End of file - total 501 lines)
</content>

<content>
1: #!/usr/bin/env node
2: // ---------------------------------------------------------------
3: // MODULE: Reindex Embeddings
4: // ---------------------------------------------------------------
5: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
6: // 1. REINDEX EMBEDDINGS
7: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
8: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
9:    1. MODULE SETUP
10: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
11: 
12: import {
13:   closeIndexingRuntime,
14:   initializeIndexingRuntime,
15:   runMemoryIndexScan,
16:   warmEmbeddingModel,
17: } from '@spec-kit/mcp-server/api/indexing';
18: 
19: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
20:    2. TYPES
21: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
22:

24: import { isMainModule } from '../lib/esm-entry.js';

26: interface ScanData {

30:   updated: number;

34:     found: number;
35:     indexed: number;
36:     alreadyIndexed: number;
37:   };

39: }
40: 
41: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
42:    3. REINDEX FUNCTION
43: ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
44: 
45: async function reindex(): Promise<void> {

70: 
71:     console.log('[4/5] Initializing search modules...');

<content>
1: // ---------------------------------------------------------------
2: // MODULE: Cleanup Orphaned Vectors
3: // ---------------------------------------------------------------
4: 
5: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
6: // 1. CLEANUP ORPHANED VECTORS
7: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
8: // Database maintenance — removes orphaned vector embeddings and history entries
9: 
10: // External packages
11: import Database from 'better-sqlite3';
12: import { load as loadSqliteVec } from 'sqlite-vec';
13: 
14: // Shared config
15: import { DB_PATH } from '@spec-kit/shared/paths';
16: import { acquireDbInstanceLock, releaseDbInstanceLocks } from '@spec-kit/mcp-server/api/db-lock';
17: import { isMainModule } from '../lib/esm-entry.js';
18: 
19: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
20:    1. INTERFACES
21: ------------------------------------------------------------------*/
22: 
23: interface CountResult {
24:   count: number;
25: }
26: 
27: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
28:    2. CONFIGURATION
29: ------------------------------------------------------------------*/
30: 
31: const dbPath: string = DB_PATH;
32: 
33: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
34:    2.1 HELP TEXT
35: ------------------------------------------------------------------*/
36: 
37: const HELP_TEXT = `
38: cleanup-orphaned-vectors — Remove orphaned vector embeddings and history entries
39: 
40: Usage: node cleanup-orphaned-vectors.js [options]
41: 
42: Options:
43:   --dry-run           Preview what would be deleted without making changes
44:   --help, -h          Show this help message
45: 
46: Examples:
47:   node cleanup-orphaned-vectors.js                # Run cleanup
48:   node cleanup-orphaned-vectors.js --dry-run      # Preview only
49: `;
50: 
51: if (process.argv.includes('--help') || process.argv.includes('-h')) {
52:   console.log(HELP_TEXT);
53:   process.exit(0);
54: }
55: 
56: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
57:    3. MAIN FUNCTION
58: ------------------------------------------------------------------*/
59: 
60: async function main(): Promise<void> {
61:   const dryRun: boolean = process.argv.includes('--dry-run');
62:   let database: InstanceType<typeof Database> | null = null;
63:   try {
64:     if (dryRun) {
65:       console.log('=== DRY-RUN MODE — no changes will be made ===\n');
66:     }
67:     console.log('Opening database:', dbPath);
68:     // Single-writer guard: refuse to mutate a database a live daemon (or
69:     // another maintenance run) currently owns — stop the daemon first.
70:     acquireDbInstanceLock(dbPath);
71:     database = new Database(dbPath);
72:     loadSqliteVec(database);
73: 
74:     let totalCleaned = 0;
75: 
76:     // STEP 1: Discover orphaned entries across all tables
77:     console.log('\n[Step 1] Finding orphaned memory_history entries...');
78:     let orphanedHistoryCount = 0;
79:     let hasHistoryTable = true;
80:     try {
81:       const orphanedHistory = database.prepare(`
82:         SELECT COUNT(*) as count
83:         FROM memory_history h
84:         LEFT JOIN memory_index m ON h.memory_id = m.id
85:         WHERE m.id IS NULL
86:       `).get() as CountResult;
87: 
88:       orphanedHistoryCount = orphanedHistory.count;
89: 
90:       if (orphanedHistoryCount > 0) {
91:         console.log(`Found ${orphanedHistoryCount} orphaned history entries`);
92:       } else {
93:         console.log('No orphaned history entries found');
94:       }
95:     } catch (e: unknown) {
96:       const errMsg = e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e);
97:       if (errMsg.includes('no such table')) {
98:         hasHistoryTable = false;
99:       } else {
100:         console.warn('memory_history discovery warning:', errMsg);
101:       }
102:     }
103: 
104:     console.log('\n[Step 2] Finding orphaned vector entries...');
105:     const orphanedVectors = database.prepare(`
106:       SELECT COUNT(*) as count
107:       FROM vec_memories v
108:       LEFT JOIN memory_index m ON v.rowid = m.id
109:       WHERE m.id IS NULL
110:     `).get() as CountResult;
111: 
112:     const orphanedVectorCount = orphanedVectors.count;
113:     console.log(`Found ${orphanedVectorCount} orphaned vectors`);
114: 
115:     // STEP 2: Delete all orphans in a single atomic transaction
116:     // ISS-B04-002 fix — wrapping history + vector cleanup in one
117:     // Transaction prevents partial commits on mid-run failure.
118:     if (dryRun) {
119:       if (orphanedHistoryCount > 0) {
120:         console.log(`[DRY-RUN] Would delete ${orphanedHistoryCount} orphaned history entries`);
121:         totalCleaned += orphanedHistoryCount;
122:       }
123:       if (orphanedVectorCount > 0) {
124:         console.log(`[DRY-RUN] Would delete ${orphanedVectorCount} orphaned vectors`);
125:         totalCleaned += orphanedVectorCount;
126:       }
127:     } else if (orphanedHistoryCount > 0 || orphanedVectorCount > 0) {
128:       console.log('\n[Step 3] Deleting all orphans in a single atomic transaction...');
129: 
130:       const atomicCleanup = database.transaction(() => {
131:         if (!database) throw new Error('Database connection lost');
132: 
133:         // Delete orphaned history entries
134:         if (hasHistoryTable && orphanedHistoryCount > 0) {
135:           const historyDeleteResult = database.prepare(`
136:             DELETE FROM memory_history
137:             WHERE memory_id NOT IN (SELECT id FROM memory_index)
138:           `).run();
139:           console.log(`Deleted ${historyDeleteResult.changes} orphaned history entries`);
140:           totalCleaned += historyDeleteResult.changes;
141:         }
142: 
143:         // Delete orphaned vector entries
144:         if (orphanedVectorCount > 0) {
145:           const vectorDeleteResult = database.prepare(`
146:             DELETE FROM vec_memories
147:             WHERE rowid NOT IN (SELECT id FROM memory_index)
148:           `).run();
149:           console.log(`Deleted ${vectorDeleteResult.changes} orphaned vectors`);
150:           totalCleaned += vectorDeleteResult.changes;
151:         }
152:       });
153: 
154:       atomicCleanup();
155:       console.log(`Atomic cleanup committed: ${totalCleaned} total entries removed`);
156:     }
157: 
158:     // STEP 4: Verify and report
159:     console.log('\n[Step 4] Verification...');
160:     const memoryCount: CountResult = database.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM memory_index').get() as CountResult;
161:     const vectorCount: CountResult = database.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM vec_memories').get() as CountResult;
162: 
163:     let historyCount: CountResult = { count: 0 };
164:     try {
165:       historyCount = database.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM memory_history').get() as CountResult;
166:     } catch (_e: unknown) {
167:       if (_e instanceof Error) {
168:         // Table may not exist
169:       }
170:     }
171: 
172:     console.log('\nFinal counts:');
173:     console.log(`  Memories: ${memoryCount.count}`);
174:     console.log(`  Vectors:  ${vectorCount.count}`);
175:     console.log(`  History:  ${historyCount.count}`);
176:     console.log(`\nTotal cleaned: ${totalCleaned}${dryRun ? ' (dry-run, nothing actually deleted)' : ''}`);
177: 
178:     database.close();
179:     releaseDbInstanceLocks();
180:     console.log(`\nCleanup ${dryRun ? 'preview' : 'completed'} successfully`);
181:     process.exit(0);
182:   } catch (error: unknown) {
183:     const errMsg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
184:     console.error('[cleanup-orphaned-vectors] Error:', errMsg);
185:     if (database) {
186:       try {
187:         database.close();
188:       } catch (_closeErr: unknown) {
189:         if (_closeErr instanceof Error) {
190:           // Ignore close errors
191:         }
192:       }
193:     }
194:     releaseDbInstanceLocks();
195:     process.exit(1);
196:   }
197: }
198: 
199: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
200:    4. INITIALIZE
201: ------------------------------------------------------------------*/
202: 
203: if (isMainModule(import.meta.url)) {
204:   main();
205: }
206: 
207: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
208:    5. EXPORTS
209: ------------------------------------------------------------------*/
210: 
211: export { main };

(End of file - total 211 lines)
</content>

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: #!/usr/bin/env node
2: // ---------------------------------------------------------------
3: // MODULE: Rank Memories
4: // ---------------------------------------------------------------
5: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
6: // 1. RANK MEMORIES
7: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
8: // Computes composite ranking scores for memories and folders with recency decay
9: 
10: import * as fs from 'fs';
11: 
12: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
13:    1. IMPORTS FROM FOLDER-SCORING
14: ------------------------------------------------------------------*/
15: 
16: import {
17:   isArchived,
18:   getArchiveMultiplier,
19:   computeRecencyScore,
20:   computeSingleFolderScore,
21:   simplifyFolderPath,
22:   findTopTier,
23:   findLastActivity,
24:   TIER_WEIGHTS,
25:   SCORE_WEIGHTS,
26:   DECAY_RATE,
27: } from '@spec-kit/shared/scoring/folder-scoring';
28: import type { FolderMemoryInput } from '@spec-kit/shared/scoring/folder-scoring';
29: import { isMainModule } from '../lib/esm-entry.js';
30: 
31: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
32:    2. INTERFACES
33: ------------------------------------------------------------------*/
34: 
35: /** Raw memory record read from input data. */
36: export interface RawMemory {
37:   id?: number;
38:   title?: string;
39:   specFolder?: string;
40:   importanceTier?: string;
41:   createdAt?: string;
42:   updatedAt?: string;
43:   filePath?: string | null;
44:   triggerCount?: number;
45:   importanceWeight?: number;
46: }
47: 
48: /** Memory record normalized for ranking and reporting. */
49: export interface NormalizedMemory {
50:   id: string | number;
51:   title: string;
52:   specFolder: string;
53:   importanceTier: string;
54:   createdAt: string;
55:   updatedAt: string;
56:   filePath: string | null;
57:   triggerCount: number;
58:   importanceWeight: number;
59:   /** Index signature for FolderMemoryInput compatibility (Record<string, unknown>) */
60:   [key: string]: unknown;
61: }
62: 
63: /** Ranked constitutional memory entry included in output summaries. */
64: export interface ConstitutionalEntry {
65:   id: string | number;
66:   title: string;
67:   specFolder: string;
68:   simplified: string;
69: }
70: 
71: /** Folder-level aggregate score entry used in ranking output. */
72: export interface FolderScoreEntry {
73:   folder: string;
74:   simplified: string;
75:   score: number;
76:   memoryCount: number;
77:   lastUpdate: string;
78:   lastUpdateRelative: string;
79:   topTier: string;
80:   isArchived: boolean;
81: }
82: 
83: /** Recent memory entry included in ranking summaries. */
84: export interface RecentMemoryEntry {
85:   id: string | number;
86:   title: string;
87:   specFolder: string;
88:   simplified: string;
89:   updatedAt: string;
90:   updatedAtRelative: string;
91:   tier: string;
92: }
93: 
94: /** Aggregate result returned from ranking memory records. */
95: export interface ProcessingResult {
96:   constitutional: ConstitutionalEntry[];
97:   recentlyActive: FolderScoreEntry[];
98:   highImportance: FolderScoreEntry[];
99:   recentMemories: RecentMemoryEntry[];
100:   stats: {
101:     totalMemories: number;
102:     totalFolders: number;
103:     activeFolders: number;
104:     archivedFolders: number;
105:     showingArchived: boolean;
106:   };
107: }
108: 
109: /** Options controlling memory ranking and output generation. */
110: export interface ProcessingOptions {
111:   showArchived?: boolean;
112:   folderLimit?: number;
113:   memoryLimit?: number;
114: }
115: 
116: /** CLI options accepted by the memory ranking script. */
117: export interface CLIOptions extends ProcessingOptions {
118:   format?: string;
119:   filePath?: string | null;
120: }
121: 
122: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
123:    3. HELPER FUNCTIONS
124: ------------------------------------------------------------------*/
125: 
126: function formatRelativeTime(timestamp: string): string {
127:   const date = new Date(timestamp);
128:   if (isNaN(date.getTime())) {
129:     return 'unknown';
130:   }
131: 
132:   const daysSince = (Date.now() - date.getTime()) / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24);
133: 
134:   if (daysSince < 0) return 'future';
135:   if (daysSince < 1 / 24) return 'just now';
136:   if (daysSince < 1) return `${Math.floor(daysSince * 24)}h ago`;
137:   if (daysSince < 7) return `${Math.floor(daysSince)}d ago`;
138:   if (daysSince < 30) return `${Math.floor(daysSince / 7)}w ago`;
139:   if (daysSince < 365) return `${Math.floor(daysSince / 30)}mo ago`;
140:   return `${Math.floor(daysSince / 365)}y ago`;
141: }
142: 
143: function computeFolderScore(folderPath: string, folderMemories: NormalizedMemory[]): number {
144:   // NormalizedMemory is structurally compatible with FolderMemoryInput (id: string|number vs string is the only diff)
145:   const result = computeSingleFolderScore(folderPath, folderMemories as FolderMemoryInput[]);
146:   return result.score;
147: }
148: 
149: function normalizeMemory(m: RawMemory): NormalizedMemory {
150:   return {
151:     id: m.id ?? 0,
152:     title: m.title ?? 'Untitled',
153:     specFolder: m.specFolder ?? 'unknown',
154:     importanceTier: m.importanceTier ?? 'normal',
155:     createdAt: m.createdAt ?? new Date().toISOString(),
156:     updatedAt: m.updatedAt ?? m.createdAt ?? new Date().toISOString(),
157:     filePath: m.filePath ?? null,
158:     triggerCount: m.triggerCount ?? 0,
159:     importanceWeight: m.importanceWeight ?? 0.5
160:   };
161: }
162: 
163: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
164:    4. MAIN PROCESSING
165: ------------------------------------------------------------------*/
166: 
167: function processMemories(rawMemories: RawMemory[], options: ProcessingOptions = {}): ProcessingResult {
168:   const {
169:     showArchived = false,
170:     folderLimit = 3,
171:     memoryLimit = 5
172:   } = options;
173: 
174:   const memories: NormalizedMemory[] = rawMemories.map(normalizeMemory);
175: 
176:   const constitutional: ConstitutionalEntry[] = memories
177:     .filter((m) => m.importanceTier === 'constitutional')
178:     .slice(0, folderLimit)
179:     .map((m) => ({
180:       id: m.id,
181:       title: m.title,
182:       specFolder: m.specFolder,
183:       simplified: simplifyFolderPath(m.specFolder)
184:     }));
185: 
186:   const folderMap = new Map<string, NormalizedMemory[]>();
187:   for (const memory of memories) {
188:     const folder = memory.specFolder || 'unknown';
189:     if (!folderMap.has(folder)) {
190:       folderMap.set(folder, []);
191:     }
192:     const folderList = folderMap.get(folder);
193:     if (folderList) folderList.push(memory);
194:   }
195: 
196:   const folderScores: FolderScoreEntry[] = [];
197:   for (const [folder, folderMemories] of folderMap) {
198:     const isArchivedFolder: boolean = isArchived(folder);
199: 
200:     if (isArchivedFolder && !showArchived) continue;
201: 
202:     const score: number = computeFolderScore(folder, folderMemories);
203:     const topTier: string = findTopTier(folderMemories as FolderMemoryInput[]);
204:     const lastActivity: string = findLastActivity(folderMemories as FolderMemoryInput[]);
205: 
206:     folderScores.push({
207:       folder,
208:       simplified: simplifyFolderPath(folder),
209:       score: Math.round(score * 1000) / 1000,
210:       memoryCount: folderMemories.length,
211:       lastUpdate: lastActivity,
212:       lastUpdateRelative: formatRelativeTime(lastActivity),
213:       topTier: topTier,
214:       isArchived: isArchivedFolder
215:     });
216:   }
217: 
218:   folderScores.sort((a, b) => b.score - a.score);
219: 
220:   const highImportance: FolderScoreEntry[] = folderScores
221:     .filter((f) => f.topTier === 'critical' || f.topTier === 'constitutional')
222:     .filter((f) => !f.isArchived)
223:     .slice(0, folderLimit);
224: 
225:   const recentlyActive: FolderScoreEntry[] = folderScores
226:     .filter((f) => !f.isArchived)
227:     .slice(0, folderLimit);
228: 
229:   const recentMemories: RecentMemoryEntry[] = [...memories]
230:     .filter((m) => m.importanceTier !== 'constitutional')
231:     .filter((m) => showArchived || !isArchived(m.specFolder))
232:     .sort((a, b) => new Date(b.updatedAt).getTime() - new Date(a.updatedAt).getTime())
233:     .slice(0, memoryLimit)
234:     .map((m) => ({
235:       id: m.id,
236:       title: m.title,
237:       specFolder: m.specFolder,
238:       simplified: simplifyFolderPath(m.specFolder),
239:       updatedAt: m.updatedAt,
240:       updatedAtRelative: formatRelativeTime(m.updatedAt),
241:       tier: m.importanceTier || 'normal'
242:     }));
243: 
244:   const allFolders: Array<{ folder: string; isArchived: boolean }> = [];
245:   for (const [folder] of folderMap) {
246:     allFolders.push({
247:       folder,
248:       isArchived: isArchived(folder)
249:     });
250:   }
251:   const archivedCount: number = allFolders.filter((f) => f.isArchived).length;
252: 
253:   return {
254:     constitutional,
255:     recentlyActive,
256:     highImportance,
257:     recentMemories,
258:     stats: {
259:       totalMemories: memories.length,
260:       totalFolders: folderMap.size,
261:       activeFolders: folderMap.size - archivedCount,
262:       archivedFolders: archivedCount,
263:       showingArchived: showArchived
264:     }
265:   };
266: }
267: 
268: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
269:    5. CLI INTERFACE
270: ------------------------------------------------------------------*/
271: 
272: function readStdin(): Promise<string> {
273:   return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
274:     let data = '';
275:     process.stdin.setEncoding('utf8');
276: 
277:     if (process.stdin.isTTY) {
278:       resolve('');
279:       return;
280:     }
281: 
282:     process.stdin.on('readable', () => {
283:       let chunk: string | null;
284:       while ((chunk = process.stdin.read() as string | null) !== null) {
285:         data += chunk;
286:       }
287:     });
288: 
289:     process.stdin.on('end', () => resolve(data));
290:     process.stdin.on('error', reject);
291:   });
292: }
293: 
294: function parseArgs(args: string[]): CLIOptions {
295:   const options: CLIOptions = {
296:     showArchived: false,
297:     folderLimit: 3,
298:     memoryLimit: 5,
299:     format: 'full',
300:     filePath: null
301:   };
302: 
303:   for (let i = 0; i < args.length; i++) {
304:     const arg = args[i];
305: 
306:     if (arg === '--show-archived') {
307:       options.showArchived = true;
308:     } else if (arg === '--folder-limit' && args[i + 1]) {
309:       options.folderLimit = parseInt(args[++i], 10) || 3;
310:     } else if (arg === '--memory-limit' && args[i + 1]) {
311:       options.memoryLimit = parseInt(args[++i], 10) || 5;
312:     } else if (arg === '--format' && args[i + 1]) {
313:       options.format = args[++i];
314:     } else if (arg.startsWith('--file=')) {
315:       options.filePath = arg.slice(7);
316:     } else if (!arg.startsWith('--') && (arg.endsWith('.json') || fs.existsSync(arg))) {
317:       options.filePath = arg;
318:     }
319:   }
320: 
321:   return options;
322: }
323: 
324: function showHelp(): void {
325:   console.log(`
326: rank-memories.js - Compute composite ranking scores for memories and folders
327: 
328: Usage:
329:   cat memories.json | node rank-memories.js [options]
330:   node rank-memories.js /path/to/memories.json [options]
331: 
332: Options:
333:   --show-archived     Include archived folders in output
334:   --folder-limit N    Max folders per section (default: 3)
335:   --memory-limit N    Max recent memories (default: 5)
336:   --format compact    Output format (compact|full)
337:   --help, -h          Show this help
338: `);
339: }
340: 
341: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
342:    6. ENTRY POINT
343: ------------------------------------------------------------------*/
344: 
345: async function main(): Promise<void> {
346:   const args = process.argv.slice(2);
347: 
348:   if (args.includes('--help') || args.includes('-h')) {
349:     showHelp();
350:     process.exit(0);
351:   }
352: 
353:   const options = parseArgs(args);
354: 
355:   let inputData: string;
356:   try {
357:     if (options.filePath) {
358:       inputData = fs.readFileSync(options.filePath, 'utf8');
359:     } else {
360:       inputData = await readStdin();
361:     }
362:   } catch (err: unknown) {
363:     const errMsg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
364:     console.error(`Error reading input: ${errMsg}`);
365:     process.exit(1);
366:   }
367: 
368:   if (!inputData || inputData.trim() === '') {
369:     console.error('Error: No input data provided. Pipe JSON or specify a file path.');
370:     console.error('Run with --help for usage information.');
371:     process.exit(1);
372:   }
373: 
374:   let parsedMemories: RawMemory[] | null = null;
375:   try {
376:     const parsed = JSON.parse(inputData) as unknown;
377:     if (Array.isArray(parsed)) {
378:       parsedMemories = parsed as RawMemory[];
379:     } else if (
380:       typeof parsed === 'object' &&
381:       parsed !== null &&
382:       'results' in parsed &&
383:       Array.isArray((parsed as { results?: unknown }).results)
384:     ) {
385:       parsedMemories = (parsed as { results: RawMemory[] }).results;
386:     } else {
387:       throw new Error('Input JSON must be an array or an object with a results array');
388:     }
389:   } catch (err: unknown) {
390:     const errMsg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
391:     console.error(`Error parsing JSON: ${errMsg}`);
392:     process.exit(1);
393:     return;
394:   }
395: 
396:   if (!parsedMemories) {
397:     return;
398:   }
399: 
400:   const memories = parsedMemories;
401:   const result = processMemories(memories, {
402:     showArchived: options.showArchived,
403:     folderLimit: options.folderLimit,
404:     memoryLimit: options.memoryLimit
405:   });
406: 
407:   if (options.format === 'compact') {
408:     console.log(JSON.stringify(result));
409:   } else {
410:     console.log(JSON.stringify(result, null, 2));
411:   }
412: }
413: 
414: // Guard main() execution to prevent side effects on import
415: if (isMainModule(import.meta.url)) {
416:   main().catch((err: unknown) => {
417:     const errMsg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
418:     console.error(`Error: ${errMsg}`);
419:     process.exit(1);
420:   });
421: }
422: 
423: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
424:    7. EXPORTS
425: ------------------------------------------------------------------*/
426: 
427: export {
428:   // Re-exported from folder-scoring
429:   isArchived,
430:   getArchiveMultiplier,
431:   computeRecencyScore,
432:   simplifyFolderPath,
433:   TIER_WEIGHTS,
434:   SCORE_WEIGHTS,
435:   DECAY_RATE,

440: };

<content>
1: #!/usr/bin/env node
2: // One-shot repair script: Fix H1 headings in all memory files.
3: //
4: // For each .md file under .opencode/specs/**/memory/ -
5: //   1. Extract the slug from the filename (everything after __, before .md)
6: //   2. Convert slug to title case
7: //   3. Replace existing H1 (or insert one if missing) with the title-cased slug
8: //   4. Ensure a blank line exists between closing --- and # H1
9: //
10: // Usage: node fix-memory-h1.mjs [--dry-run]
11: 
12: import fs from 'node:fs';
13: import path from 'node:path';
14: 
15: const SPECS_ROOT = path.resolve(import.meta.dirname, '..', '..', '..', '..', 'specs');
16: const DRY_RUN = process.argv.includes('--dry-run');
17: 
18: function slugToTitle(slug) {
19:   return slug
20:     .replace(/(?<=\d)-(?=\d)/g, '\x00')   // protect digit-digit hyphens (dates like 2026-03-13)
21:     .replace(/-/g, ' ')
22:     .replace(/\x00/g, '-')                 // restore digit-digit hyphens
23:     .replace(/\s{2,}/g, ' ')              // collapse consecutive spaces
24:     .replace(/\b\w/g, (ch) => ch.toUpperCase());
25: }
26: 
27: function extractSlugFromFilename(filename) {
28:   const stem = path.basename(filename, '.md');
29:   const doubleUnderIdx = stem.indexOf('__');
30:   if (doubleUnderIdx === -1) return null;
31:   return stem.slice(doubleUnderIdx + 2);
32: }
33: 
34: function findMemoryFiles(dir) {
35:   const results = [];
36:   for (const entry of fs.readdirSync(dir, { withFileTypes: true })) {
37:     const full = path.join(dir, entry.name);
38:     if (entry.isDirectory()) {
39:       if (entry.name === 'quarantine' || entry.name === 'scratch') continue;
40:       results.push(...findMemoryFiles(full));
41:     } else if (entry.isFile() && entry.name.endsWith('.md') && path.basename(path.dirname(full)) === 'memory') {
42:       results.push(full);
43:     }
44:   }
45:   return results;
46: }
47: 
48: function fixFile(filePath) {
49:   const slug = extractSlugFromFilename(filePath);
50:   if (!slug) {
51:     return { path: filePath, status: 'skipped', reason: 'no slug in filename' };
52:   }
53: 
54:   const title = slugToTitle(slug);
55:   const content = fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf-8');
56: 
57:   // Parse frontmatter
58:   const fmMatch = content.match(/^(---\r?\n[\s\S]*?\r?\n---)([\s\S]*)$/);
59:   if (!fmMatch) {
60:     return { path: filePath, status: 'skipped', reason: 'no frontmatter' };
61:   }
62: 
63:   const frontmatter = fmMatch[1];
64:   let body = fmMatch[2];
65: 
66:   // Remove existing H1 if present (with or without blank line before it)
67:   body = body.replace(/^\s*#\s+[^\n]*\n?/, '');
68: 
69:   // Ensure body starts with a blank line, then H1, then blank line
70:   body = body.replace(/^\n*/, '');
71:   const newBody = `\n\n# ${title}\n\n${body}`;
72: 
73:   const newContent = frontmatter + newBody;
74: 
75:   if (newContent === content) {
76:     return { path: filePath, status: 'unchanged' };
77:   }
78: 
79:   if (!DRY_RUN) {
80:     fs.writeFileSync(filePath, newContent, 'utf-8');
81:   }
82: 
83:   return { path: filePath, status: 'fixed', title };
84: }
85: 
86: // Main
87: const files = findMemoryFiles(SPECS_ROOT);
88: console.log(`Found ${files.length} memory files under ${SPECS_ROOT}`);
89: if (DRY_RUN) console.log('DRY RUN — no files will be modified\n');
90: 
91: let fixed = 0;
92: let unchanged = 0;
93: let skipped = 0;
94: 
95: for (const f of files) {
96:   const result = fixFile(f);
97:   if (result.status === 'fixed') {
98:     fixed++;
99:     console.log(`  FIXED: ${path.relative(SPECS_ROOT, result.path)} → # ${result.title}`);
100:   } else if (result.status === 'unchanged') {
101:     unchanged++;
102:   } else {
103:     skipped++;
104:     console.log(`  SKIP:  ${path.relative(SPECS_ROOT, result.path)} (${result.reason})`);
105:   }
106: }
107: 
108: console.log(`\nDone: ${fixed} fixed, ${unchanged} unchanged, ${skipped} skipped`);

(End of file - total 108 lines)
</content>

1: // ---------------------------------------------------------------
2: // MODULE: Rebuild Auto Entities
3: // ---------------------------------------------------------------
4: 
5: import Database from 'better-sqlite3';
6: 
7: import { DB_PATH } from '@spec-kit/shared/paths';
8: import { rebuildAutoEntities } from '@spec-kit/mcp-server/api';
9: import { acquireDbInstanceLock, releaseDbInstanceLocks } from '@spec-kit/mcp-server/api/db-lock';
10: import { isMainModule } from '../lib/esm-entry.js';
11: 
12: interface ParsedArgs {
13:   dryRun: boolean;
14:   specFolder: string | null;
15: }
16: 
17: interface RebuildAutoEntitiesResult {
18:   dryRun: boolean;
19:   specFolder: string | null;
20:   memoriesScanned: number;
21:   memoriesReprocessed: number;
22:   autoRowsRemoved: number;
23:   extractedEntities: number;
24:   storedEntities: number;
25:   catalogEntriesRebuilt: number;
26: }
27: 
28: 
29: const HELP_TEXT = `
30: rebuild-auto-entities — Rebuild auto-generated entity rows from current memory content
31: 
32: Usage: node rebuild-auto-entities.js [options]
33: 
34: Options:
35:   --dry-run                 Preview the rebuild without changing the database
36:   --spec-folder <path>      Limit the rebuild to one spec folder
37:   --help, -h                Show this help message
38: 
39: Examples:
40:   node rebuild-auto-entities.js --dry-run
41:   node rebuild-auto-entities.js --spec-folder specs/123-example
42: `;
43: 
44: function parseArgs(argv: string[]): ParsedArgs {
45:   if (argv.includes('--help') || argv.includes('-h')) {
46:     console.log(HELP_TEXT);
47:     process.exit(0);
48:   }
49: 
50:   const dryRun = argv.includes('--dry-run');
51:   const specFolderIndex = argv.indexOf('--spec-folder');
52:   let specFolder: string | null = null;
53: 
54:   if (specFolderIndex !== -1) {
55:     const value = argv[specFolderIndex + 1];
56:     if (!value || value.startsWith('--')) {
57:       throw new Error('Expected a spec folder path after --spec-folder');
58:     }
59:     specFolder = value.trim();
60:   }
61: 
62:   return { dryRun, specFolder };
63: }
64: 
65: async function main(): Promise<void> {
66:   const args = parseArgs(process.argv.slice(2));
67:   // Single-writer guard: refuse to mutate a database a live daemon (or
68:   // another maintenance run) currently owns — stop the daemon first.
69:   acquireDbInstanceLock(DB_PATH);
70:   const database = new Database(DB_PATH);
71: 
72:   try {
73:     const result = rebuildAutoEntities(database, args);
74: 
75:     console.log(args.dryRun ? '=== DRY-RUN MODE — no changes were made ===' : '=== AUTO ENTITIES REBUILT ===');
76:     console.log(`Database: ${DB_PATH}`);
77:     console.log(`Scope: ${result.specFolder ?? 'all spec folders'}`);
78:     console.log(`Memories scanned: ${result.memoriesScanned}`);
79:     console.log(`Memories reprocessed: ${result.memoriesReprocessed}`);
80:     console.log(`Auto rows removed: ${result.autoRowsRemoved}`);
81:     console.log(`Entities extracted: ${result.extractedEntities}`);
82:     console.log(`Entities stored: ${result.storedEntities}`);
83:     if (!args.dryRun) {
84:       console.log(`Catalog entries rebuilt: ${result.catalogEntriesRebuilt}`);
85:     }
86:   } finally {
87:     database.close();
88:     releaseDbInstanceLocks();
89:   }
90: }
91: 
92: if (isMainModule(import.meta.url)) {
93:   void main().catch((error: unknown) => {
94:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
95:     console.error('[rebuild-auto-entities] Error:', message);
96:     process.exit(1);
97:   });
98: }
99: 
100: export { main };

(End of file - total 100 lines)
</content>

4: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
5: // 1. GENERATE CONTEXT
6: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
7: // CLI entry point -- parses arguments, validates spec folder, and runs the memory workflow
