23: const CANONICAL_SAVE_STALE_MS = 30_000;
24: const CANONICAL_SAVE_HEARTBEAT_MS = 10_000;
25: const canonicalSaveHeartbeats = new Map();
26: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
27: // 3. HELP TEXT
28: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
29: const HELP_TEXT = `
30: Usage: node generate-context.js [options] <input>
31: 
32: Arguments:
33:   <input>           A JSON data file path
34:                     - JSON file mode: node generate-context.js data.json [spec-folder]
35: 
36: Options:
37:   --help, -h        Show this help message
38:   --stdin           Read structured JSON from stdin (preferred when a caller already has curated session data)
39:   --json <string>   Read structured JSON from an inline string (preferred when structured data is available)
40:   --session-id <uuid>  Explicit session ID to attach to the saved memory metadata
41:   --planner-mode <mode>  Canonical save planner mode: plan-only (default), full-auto, or hybrid
42:   --full-auto       Shortcut for --planner-mode full-auto
43: 
44: Examples:
45:   node generate-context.js ${SESSION_SCOPED_SAVE_CONTEXT_EXAMPLE}
46:   node generate-context.js ${SESSION_SCOPED_SAVE_CONTEXT_EXAMPLE} specs/001-feature/
47:   node generate-context.js ${SESSION_SCOPED_SAVE_CONTEXT_EXAMPLE} .opencode/specs/001-feature/
48:   echo '{"specFolder":"specs/001-feature/"}' | node generate-context.js --stdin
49:   node generate-context.js --json '{"specFolder":"specs/001-feature/"}'
50: 
51: Output:
52:   Creates the saved context artifact for the active spec folder and indexes it
53:   into the Spec Kit Memory system. Canonical saves also refresh the packet's
54:   root-level graph-metadata.json file as part of the same workflow.
55:   Planner-first canonical saves are requested by default; use --full-auto for
56:   the legacy mutation-first fallback.
57: 
58: Preferred save path (JSON-PRIMARY):
59:   - ALWAYS use --stdin, --json, or a JSON temp file.
60:   - If file mode is required, use a session-scoped temp path such as ${SESSION_SCOPED_SAVE_CONTEXT_EXAMPLE}.
61:   - The legacy shared path /tmp/save-context-data.json is rejected.
62:   - The AI has strictly better information about its own session than any DB query can reconstruct.
63:   - Explicit CLI targets still outrank payload specFolder values in every structured-input mode.
64: 
65: Direct CLI target rule:
66:   - When a spec folder is passed on the CLI, that explicit target is authoritative.
67:   - Session learning, JSON SPEC_FOLDER fields, and auto-detect may inform logging,
68:     but they must not reroute the save to another folder.
69: 
70: JSON Data Format (with preflight/postflight, session/git, and tool/exchange enrichment):
71:   {
72:     "user_prompts": [...],
73:     "observations": [...],
74:     "recent_context": [...],
75:     "toolCalls": [
76:       { "tool": "Read", "inputSummary": "Read data-loader.ts", "outputSummary": "585 lines", "status": "success", "durationEstimate": "fast" },
77:       { "tool": "Edit", "inputSummary": "Added deprecation warning", "outputSummary": "Inserted 10 lines", "status": "success" }
78:     ],
79:     "exchanges": [

81:     ],
82:     "preflight": {

84:       "uncertaintyScore": 60,

94:       "contextScore": 80,

96:       "newGaps": ["new-gap"]
97:     }
98:   }
99: 
100:   Tool/Exchange enrichment fields (all optional — JSON-mode only):
101:   - toolCalls[]: AI-summarized tool calls with tool name, input/output summaries, status, duration
102:   - exchanges[]: Key user-assistant exchanges with timestamps
103:   - These provide richer context than DB extraction since the AI filters noise at source
104: 
105:   Structured JSON compatibility hints (JSON-mode only):
106:   - Top-level routeAs / mergeModeHint are accepted for compatibility with routed save workflows
107:   - Top-level specDrift / reviewerFocus are accepted as optional advisory context; absence means none
108:   - Explicit CLI spec-folder targets remain authoritative over payload specFolder values
109: 
110:   Learning Delta Calculation:
111:   - Knowledge Delta = postflight.knowledgeScore - preflight.knowledgeScore
112:   - Uncertainty Reduction = preflight.uncertaintyScore - postflight.uncertaintyScore
113:   - Context Delta = postflight.contextScore - preflight.contextScore
114:   - Learning Index = (Know x 0.4) + (Uncert x 0.35) + (Context x 0.25)
115: `;

119: }
120: // 2.1 SIGNAL HANDLERS
121: let signalHandlersInstalled = false;
122: let shuttingDown = false;
123: // Robustness: signal handler releases locks before reporting
124: function handleSignalShutdown(signal) {
125:     if (shuttingDown)
126:         return; // prevent re-entrant handling
127:     shuttingDown = true;
128:     let lockReleaseFailed = false;
129:     try {
130:         releaseFilesystemLock();
131:     }
132:     catch (_err) {
133:         lockReleaseFailed = true;
134:     }
135:     console.error(`\nWarning: Received ${signal} signal, shutting down gracefully...`);
136:     process.exit(lockReleaseFailed ? 1 : 0);
137: }
138: function installSignalHandlers() {
139:     if (signalHandlersInstalled) {
140:         return;
141:     }
142:     process.on('SIGTERM', () => handleSignalShutdown('SIGTERM'));
143:     process.on('SIGINT', () => handleSignalShutdown('SIGINT'));
144:     signalHandlersInstalled = true;
145: }
146: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
147: // 4. SPEC FOLDER VALIDATION
148: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
149: function isUnderApprovedSpecsRoot(normalizedInput) {
150:     const resolved = path.resolve(CONFIG.PROJECT_ROOT, normalizedInput);
151:     if (fsSync.existsSync(resolved)) {
152:         return validateFilePath(resolved, getSpecsDirectories()) !== null;
153:     }
154:     return getSpecsDirectories().some((specsDir) => {
155:         const relative = path.relative(specsDir, resolved);
156:         return relative.length > 0 && !relative.startsWith('..') && !path.isAbsolute(relative);
157:     });
158: }
159: function isValidSpecFolder(folderPath) {
160:     const folderName = path.basename(folderPath);
161:     // --- Subfolder support: parent/child format (e.g., "003-parent/121-child" or "02--cat/003-parent/121-child") ---
162:     const normalizedInput = folderPath.replace(/\\/g, '/').replace(/\/+$/, '');
163:     // Extract the trailing portion that might be "parent/child"
164:     const trailingSegments = normalizedInput.split('/');
165:     // Check if the last two segments both match the spec folder pattern
166:     if (trailingSegments.length >= 2) {
167:         const lastTwo = trailingSegments.slice(-2);
168:         if (SPEC_FOLDER_PATTERN.test(lastTwo[0]) && SPEC_FOLDER_PATTERN.test(lastTwo[1])) {
169:             // Both segments are valid spec folder names — valid nested spec folder
170:             const hasSpecsParent = isUnderApprovedSpecsRoot(normalizedInput);
171:             if (!hasSpecsParent) {
172:                 // Fallback: check if the path resolves to an existing directory under any specs root
173:                 for (const specsDir of getSpecsDirectories()) {
174:                     const candidate = path.join(specsDir, normalizedInput);
175:                     if (!path.isAbsolute(normalizedInput) &&
176:                         fsSync.existsSync(candidate) &&
177:                         validateFilePath(candidate, getSpecsDirectories()) !== null) {
178:                         return { valid: true };
179:                     }
180:                 }
181:                 return {
182:                     valid: false,
183:                     reason: `Spec folder must be under specs/ or .opencode/specs/: ${folderPath}`
184:                 };
185:             }
186:             return { valid: true };
187:         }
188:     }
189:     if (!SPEC_FOLDER_PATTERN.test(folderName)) {
190:         if (/^\d{3}-/.test(folderName)) {
191:             if (/[A-Z]/.test(folderName)) {
192:                 return { valid: false, reason: 'Spec folder name should be lowercase' };
193:             }
194:             if (/_/.test(folderName)) {
195:                 return { valid: false, reason: 'Spec folder name should use hyphens, not underscores' };
196:             }
197:             if (!/^[a-z]/.test(folderName.slice(4))) {
198:                 return { valid: false, reason: 'Spec folder name must start with a letter after the number prefix' };
199:             }
200:         }
201:         return { valid: false, reason: 'Invalid spec folder format. Expected: NNN-feature-name' };
202:     }
203:     const hasSpecsParent = isUnderApprovedSpecsRoot(normalizedInput);
204:     if (!hasSpecsParent) {
205:         // Fallback: check if bare name can be resolved via recursive child search
206:         // (findChildFolderSync in validateArguments handles this, so just report the error here)
207:         return {
208:             valid: false,
209:             reason: `Spec folder must be under specs/ or .opencode/specs/: ${folderPath}`
210:         };
211:     }
212:     return { valid: true };
